sub_strings: This is the unit's roster number. Up to 24 units<f8>may join your party. If you dismiss a unit, that unit's<f8>number becomes available again. The next unit to<f8>join will then take on that number.
<SPEAKER>Level <8d>Lv.<8e><f8><e3>Indicates the unit's level, which increases<f8>by 1 for every 1 experience points earned.
<SPEAKER>Hit Points <8d>HP<8e><e3><f8>A measure of the unit's life. When a unit's<f8>HP is reduced to zero, he or she is KO'd.
<SPEAKER>Magick Points <8d>MP<8e><f8><e3>These points are used to cast magicks.<f8>If a unit's MP is reduced to zero, it will<f8>not be able to use any more magicks.
<SPEAKER>Charge Time <8d>CT<8e><f8><e3>Indicates the amount of time a unit must wait<f8>between turns. The unit cannot take a turn until<f8>its CT reaches 1.
<SPEAKER>Turn Order<f8><e3>Indicates a unit's turn order. The right number shows<f8>how many units are on the field, and the left number<f8>shows the selected unit's position in the turn list.
<SPEAKER>Experience Points <8d>EXP.<8e><f8><e3>A numerical representation of the unit's experience in<f8>battle. A unit's level increases by 1 for every 1<f8>experience points earned.
The unit's name.
<SPEAKER>Bravery<f8><e3>This number indicates how brave the unit is. The <f8>higher the number, the more valorous the unit is.
<SPEAKER>Faith<f8><e3>Faith represents a unit's belief in magick and spiritual<f8>phenomena. A higher number indicates deeper faith.
Currently vacant.
<SPEAKER>Move<f8><e3>A combination of the unit's base movement range<f8>and any job, equipment, or ability bonuses. The<f8>higher the number, the farther the unit can move.

<SPEAKER>Jump<f8><e3>A combination of the unit's base jumping<f8>ability and any job, equipment, or ability<f8>bonuses.<f8>This attribute governs how many tiles the<f8>unit can jump horizontally, as well as how<f8>far up or down a slope the unit can jump.
Currently vacant.
Currently vacant.
Currently vacant.
<SPEAKER>Speed<f8><e3>A combination of the unit's inherent speed and any<f8>job, equipment, or ability bonuses. A higher number<f8>means the unit's turn will come more quickly.

<SPEAKER>Attack <44> Magick <8d>ATK<8e><f8><e3>Sums of the unit's base attributes and any job, ability,<f8>or equipment bonuses. The top number is for physical<f8>attacks and the bottom number is for magickal attacks.

<SPEAKER>Weapon Attack and Block Rates <8d>Weapon Power<8e><f8><e3>The left number is the equipped weapon's attack power,<f8>and the right number indicates its chance of evasion.<f8><f8>The top and bottom numbers represent the right and<f8>left hands, respectively.
Currently vacant.

<SPEAKER>Character Attack Evasion <8d>C-EV<8e><f8><e3>A sum of the unit's base evasion rate and any job,<f8>equipment, or ability bonuses. The top and bottom values<f8>represent physical and magick attacks, respectively.
<SPEAKER>Shield Attack Evasion <8d>S-EV<8e><f8><e3>The chance a unit will block an attack with the<f8>equipped shield. The top and bottom numbers are<f8>for physical and magick attacks, respectively.

<SPEAKER>Accessory Attack Evasion  <8d>A-EV<8e><f8><e3>The chance the equipped accessory will allow the unit<f8>to evade an attack. The top and bottom numbers<f8>represent physical and magick attacks, respectively.
Indicates the tile's terrain effect bonus, which is applied<f8>to any physical effects occurring on that tile.
Indicates the tile's terrain effect bonus, which is applied<f8>to any magickal effects occurring on that tile.
An estimate of the total amount of damage<f8>or recovery the selected action will cause.
The chance of hitting a target with<f8>the selected action.
